title: The Normal Distribution
concept: The normal distribution is a symmetric bell-shaped curve where most data points cluster near the mean, and likelihood drops as you move away.

exercises.prompt: Recap: If two datasets have the same average, but Dataset A's points are much more spread out than Dataset B's, then Dataset A has a higher...?
exercises.explain: Standard deviation (and variance) measures the spread of data points around their average.

cards.title: The height of a crowd
cards.body: If you measure the height of 1,000 adults, you'll find most are close to average height. A few are very tall, and a few are very short. When plotted, this naturally forms a bell shape.

cards.title: The bell curve
cards.body: The normal distribution (or Gaussian distribution) is a symmetric probability distribution. It peaks at the mean, and its width is determined by the standard deviation.

cards.title: A dripping paint can
cards.body: Imagine a paint can dripping while hanging from a string. If it swings slightly, most drips land right underneath the center. As you walk farther left or right, you find fewer and fewer drips.

cards.title: The 68-95-99.7 rule
cards.body: In any normal distribution, about 68% of data falls within 1 standard deviation of the mean, 95% within 2, and 99.7% within 3. Anything beyond 3 is extremely rare (an outlier).

cards.title: Not all data is normal
cards.body: Many people assume all natural data fits a bell curve. But things like household income, web traffic, or city populations are highly skewed — they don't form a symmetric bell shape.

left.text:
Symmetric left and right
e.g. Human heights

right.text:
Pulled to one side by outliers
e.g. Income distribution

exercises.prompt: In a normal distribution, what percentage of data points fall within one standard deviation of the mean?
exercises.explain: The 68-95-99.7 rule dictates that approximately 68% of observations lie within one standard deviation of the average.

exercises.prompt: True or false: In a perfectly normal distribution, the mean, median, and mode are all the same value.
exercises.explain: True! Because the normal distribution is perfectly symmetric, the highest point (mode), middle value (median), and average (mean) all line up in the center.

exercises.sentence: The shape of a normal distribution is entirely defined by its mean and its ___.
exercises.explain: The mean determines the center of the bell curve, and the standard deviation determines how wide or narrow it is.

exercises.prompt: You measure exam scores and find they are normally distributed with a mean of 70 and standard deviation of 10. What score is considered an outlier (more than 3 standard deviations away)?
exercises.explain: Three standard deviations is 30 points (3 * 10). So, scores below 40 or above 100 are outliers. 105 is greater than 100, making it an outlier!

exercises.prompt: An AI system flags transaction times that are longer than normal. If transaction times follow a normal distribution with a mean of 5 seconds and a standard deviation of 1 second, when should the system raise a high-priority alert for an extreme outlier (top 0.15% of times)?

options.text: When a transaction takes more than 8 seconds (more than 3 standard deviations).
When a transaction takes exactly 5 seconds.

exercises.explain: Correct! Since 99.7% of data falls within 3 standard deviations (2 to 8 seconds), taking over 8 seconds is incredibly rare (in the top 0.15%), signaling a potential system freeze. Stellar work on the bell curve!
